dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 9431 IH: Fair Telehealth Billing Act of 2026
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Mrs. Hayes introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Education and Workforce

form.official-title: To amend the Employee Retirement Income Security Act of 1974 to prohibit health care providers and facilities from imposing certain facility fees for telehealth.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Fair Telehealth Billing Act of 2026.

section.header: Prohibiting health care providers and facilities from imposing certain facility fees for telehealth

subsection.text: Subtitle B of title I of the Employee Retirement Income Security Act of 1974 (29 U.S.C. 1021 et seq.) is amended by adding at the end the following new part:

part.header: Billing Requirements for Health Care Facilities and Providers

section.text: A health care provider or facility may not bill a separate facility fee when a health care provider who provided telehealth services is authorized to bill independently for the professional services rendered.

subsection.text: Section 502 of the Employee Retirement Income Security Act of 1974 (29 U.S.C. 1132) is amended—

paragraph.text: in subsection (a)(6), by striking or (9) and inserting (9), or (13); and
in subsection (c), by adding at the end the following new paragraph:

If a health care provider or facility is found by the Secretary to be in violation of section 901, the Secretary may assess a civil monetary penalty against such provider or facility in an amount not to exceed $10,000 per violation.

subsection.text: The table of contents of the Employee Retirement Income Security Act of 1974 (29 U.S.C. 1001 note) is amended by inserting after the item relating to section 804 the following new item:

toc.toc-entry: Part 9—Billing Requirements for Health Care Facilities and Providers

subsection.text: The amendment made by subsection (a) shall apply with respect to telehealth services furnished on or after January 1, 2028.

section.text: The Secretary of Labor shall implement the amendments made by this Act by rulemaking.
